+title: 'code-context watcher never indexes files created after startup (calcutron run: LSP/index empty)'
+---
+# code-context: incremental watcher is blind to newly-created files
+
+Investigation of the calcutron demo run (`/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/calcutron`, demo.sh `/finish` build, Jun 19 2026). Two prior agents reported "LSP never ran / all results tree-sitter / index frozen." Both got the *symptoms* right and the *cause* wrong. Here is the evidence-backed root cause.
+
+## What actually happened (reconciled)
+
+Leader election **worked**. Exactly one leader was elected: **PID 9852, `sah serve`, started Fri Jun 19 14:18:09**, still alive 24h later, still holding the flock `/var/folders/.../T/code-context-ts-bc3927cb1cd02e5607b40c650bbd4c3b.lock` (verified: PID-in-lock=9852, flock held, no sock). The other 314 processes in the run correctly became followers and polled. (The "0 leaders" count was a measurement artifact: the leader's log `mcp.9852.log` is still open, so a Jun-19-mtime filter excluded it. Logs are UTC, file mtimes local CDT: index froze 14:19:31 local = 19:19:31 UTC.)
+
+The leader **did** spawn rust-analyzer successfully and **did** index at startup:
+- `19:18:11 LSP supervisor started — 1 servers ok` / `1 LSP clients available: ["rust-analyzer"]`
+- `19:18:12 Sent didOpen for src/error.rs` → `Collected and persisted 0 symbols` / `0 LSP call edges` → `didClose`
+- `19:18:24 tree-sitter indexing complete`
+- `19:19:31 watcher: 0 dirty, 12 deleted` (the 12 scaffold .rs files were removed)
+
+Then for the **entire 3-hour /finish run** the watcher kept firing but indexed nothing:
+```
+19:19:38  16 file change(s) detected, marking dirty
+19:19:38  code-context watcher: 0 dirty, 0 deleted, 0 errors
+19:19:39  no dirty files to index
+19:20:15   2 file change(s) detected, marking dirty → 0 dirty → no dirty files to index
+... repeats to end of run
+```
+
+On-disk index DB confirms: `indexed_files`=1 (only `demo.sh`), `ts_chunks`=2 (demo.sh), `lsp_symbols`=2 (both `ts:`-prefixed = tree-sitter origin), `lsp_call_edges`=0. The 12 generated `.rs` files are absent. DB last write 14:19:31 local — frozen for the rest of the run.
+
+## Root cause (PRIMARY)
+
+The incremental watcher cannot index a file that was **not already in `indexed_files` at startup**.
+
+- `FanoutWatcher::notify` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/watcher.rs`) handles Created **and** Modified identically with **UPDATE-only** SQL:
+  `UPDATE indexed_files SET ts_indexed=0, lsp_indexed=0 WHERE file_path=?1` — **no INSERT**. For a brand-new file there is no row, so it matches **0 rows** (this is exactly what the test `test_create_event_on_unknown_file_does_not_error` asserts). → `dirty_count=0`, hence "16 detected" but "0 dirty".
+- The follow-on indexer `index_discovered_files_async` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs`) despite its name does **not** scan the filesystem. It selects `WHERE ts_indexed = 0` from existing rows only (its own doc comment: "the dirty-file query below is 'discovery'"). → "no dirty files to index".
+
+Net: only `startup_cleanup` (run once at workspace open / promotion) ever inserts rows. Any file created after that initial scan is invisible to indexing for the entire life of the (long-lived) leader. calcutron's whole calculator source was generated by `/finish` *after* startup → never indexed; and the initial scaffold was deleted at 19:19:31 and its re-creation was never picked up.
+
+## Secondary issue
+
+rust-analyzer was queried ~1s after spawn (19:18:11 → 19:18:12), before it warms up (cargo metadata / proc-macro / project load), so it returned **0 symbols / 0 call edges**, persisted them, and never retried. Even files that *are* in the index would carry empty LSP data. `lsp_symbols` would stay tree-sitter-only and `lsp_call_edges` empty until a warmup-aware re-drive. Needs: gate first LSP collection on RA readiness (`$/progress` end / workDoneProgress) or retry-on-empty, and re-drive `lsp_indexed=0` after warmup.
+
+## Minor (not a bug)
+
+The three `get_blastradius` errors (`invalid regex pattern: no symbols ... matching '*'`) are agent misuse — `*` passed as a symbol pattern. Recoverable. Optional ergonomic fix: treat `*`/empty as "all symbols in file."
+
+## Proposed fix direction (for discussion)
+
+1. **Watcher Created → INSERT-or-mark.** On a Created event (or any event where the path exists on disk but has no row), insert an `indexed_files` row with `ts_indexed=0, lsp_indexed=0` so it enters the dirty set; or have the watcher-triggered path run the same filesystem discovery/upsert that `startup_cleanup` uses, rather than only re-querying dirty rows. Add a regression test that creates a *new* file (not pre-inserted) and asserts it gets indexed.
+2. **LSP warmup gate / re-drive** (secondary) per above.
+
+## Repro / verification anchors
+- Lock: `/var/folders/l7/.../T/code-context-ts-bc3927cb1cd02e5607b40c650bbd4c3b.lock` (held by 9852).
+- Leader log: `calcutron/.sah/mcp.9852.log` (grep `marking dirty` / `no dirty files to index`).
+- Index DB: `calcutron/.code-context/index.db` (`indexed_files`, `ts_chunks`, `lsp_symbols`, `lsp_call_edges`).
+- Code: `watcher.rs::FanoutWatcher::notify`; `code_context/watcher.rs::process_file_events`; `code_context/mod.rs::index_discovered_files_with_embedder` (`WHERE ts_indexed = 0`).
\ No newline at end of file
